meta.name: BBQ Cole Slaw (Creamy + Tangy, Cookout Classic)

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic BBQ side: crisp cabbage + creamy tang dressing.
Designed to pair with brisket, ribs, pulled pork, smoked chicken, and sausages.
Includes a vinegar-forward variation for Carolina-style plates.

yield.notes: About 10 side servings (roughly 3/4 cup each) or 12 smaller servings for topping sandwiches.

ingredients.label: Green cabbage, finely shredded
ingredients.notes: About 1 medium head. Shred thin for best texture.

ingredients.label: Red cabbage, finely shredded (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds color and crunch. Use more green cabbage if skipping.

ingredients.label: Onion, very finely minced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use sweet onion or scallions for a softer bite.

ingredients.label: Mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: Start with 3/4 cup; you can add more after chilling if needed.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: For tang. White vinegar works if needed.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances vinegar. Increase to 2 tbsp for a sweeter slaw.

ingredients.label: Yellow mustard
ingredients.notes: Optional, but classic in BBQ slaw.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Used for salting cabbage and in dressing; adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Milk or buttermilk (optional, to loosen dressing)
ingredients.notes: Add only if you want a thinner dressing.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (optional)
ingredients.notes: Brightens; optional if vinegar is enough.

steps.title: Shred cabbage and prep vegetables
steps.body: Shred green cabbage (and red cabbage if using) very thin. Grate carrots. Mince onion/scallions if using. Add everything to a large bowl.

steps.title: Lightly salt cabbage (optional but recommended)
steps.body: Sprinkle 1/2 tsp of the salt over the cabbage mixture and toss. Let sit 10–15 minutes to draw out a bit of moisture (improves crunch). If a lot of liquid collects, you can drain it or blot lightly—do not fully wring dry.

steps.title: Whisk the dressing
steps.body: In a small bowl, whisk mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar (if using), mustard (if using), celery seed (if using), black pepper, remaining salt (start with 1/2 tsp), and optional lemon juice/hot sauce. If you want a thinner dressing, whisk in milk/buttermilk 1 tbsp at a time.

steps.title: Toss slaw and chill
steps.body: Pour dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss thoroughly until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour (best 2–4 hours) for flavors to meld. Taste and adjust: add vinegar for tang, sugar for sweetness, salt/pepper as needed.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve cold as a side with BBQ plates, or pile onto pulled pork sandwiches, BBQ chicken sandwiches, or hot links. Stir before serving—slaw can weep a little as it sits.

refrigerate.instructions: Keep refrigerated in a sealed container. Stir before serving.

storage.notes: Cole slaw is best within 24–48 hours for maximum crunch; it softens over time.

serveModes.label: Side Dish
serveModes.notes: Serve 3/4 cup per person as a side.

serveModes.label: Sandwich Topping
serveModes.notes: Serve on pulled pork/chicken sandwiches for crunch + tang.

serveModes.label: BBQ Plate
serveModes.notes: Pairs best with rich smoked meats; tang balances fat.

notes: Carolina vinegar slaw (no mayo): replace mayo with 1/3 cup oil, increase vinegar to 1/4 cup, add 1–2 tbsp sugar, and season with salt/pepper + red pepper flakes. Chill 1–2 hours.
Sweeter deli slaw: increase sugar to 2 tbsp and add 1–2 tbsp milk to loosen dressing.
Heat: add jalapeño mince or extra hot sauce; keep it subtle so it complements smoked meats.
For extra crisp slaw: salt-rest cabbage, then drain excess liquid before dressing.
For finer texture: use a food processor slicing disk (pulse carefully to avoid mush).
